components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: base64ct
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of Base64 (RFC 4648) which avoids any usages of data-dependent branches/LUTs and thereby provides portable "best effort" constant-time operation and embedded-friendly no_std support

components.name: block-buffer

components.author: Alex Crichton <alex@alexcrichton.com>
components.name: cfg-if
components.description: A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg] parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the item that gets emitted.

components.name: clap
components.description: A simple to use, efficient, and full-featured Command Line Argument Parser

components.description: A simple to use, efficient, and full-featured Command Line Argument Parser

components.description: Parse command line argument by defining a struct, derive crate.

components.description: Minimal, flexible command line parser

components.name: colorchoice
components.description: Global override of color control

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: const-oid
components.description: Const-friendly implementation of the ISO/IEC Object Identifier (OID) standard as defined in ITU X.660, with support for BER/DER encoding/decoding as well as heapless no_std (i.e. embedded) support

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: cpufeatures
components.description: Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets,  with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

components.description: A pure-Rust implementation of group operations on ristretto255 and Curve25519

components.name: der
components.description: Pure Rust embedded-friendly implementation of the Distinguished Encoding Rules (DER) for Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) as described in ITU X.690 with full support for heapless no_std targets

components.name: digest

components.name: libc

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: pem-rfc7468
components.description: PEM Encoding (RFC 7468) for PKIX, PKCS, and CMS Structures, implementing a strict subset of the original Privacy-Enhanced Mail encoding intended specifically for use with cryptographic keys, certificates, and other messages. Provides a no_std-friendly, constant-time implementation suitable for use with cryptographic private keys.

components.name: pkcs8

components.name: syn

components.name: typenum
components.description: Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at     compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed     integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its     implementation is incomplete.

components.name: zerocopy
components.description: Zerocopy makes zero-cost memory manipulation effortless. We write "unsafe" so you don't have to.

components.author: The RustCrypto Project Developers
components.name: zeroize
components.description: Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler. Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere, even WASM!

components.author: The RustCrypto Project Developers
components.name: zeroize_derive
components.description: Custom derive support for zeroize
